subject: Christian Religious Studies (May not be taken with Islamic Religious Studies and/or West African Traditional Religion)
aims_and_objectives: have knowledge, understanding and appreciation of the religious, cultural, historical and social background of the biblical materials prescribed to be studied;

understand the nature of biblical moral lessons in the selected passages/themes from the Bible and the extent of their relevance and possible application to present day social, political, religious and cultural life and existence;
identify difficulties involved in the application of healthy attitudes, concepts and skills acquired from the Bible in ensuring peaceful co-existence and national development;
demonstrate adequate preparation for higher education through critical thinking and independent work.

scheme_of_examination.description: There will be two papers, Papers 1 and 2, both of which will be a composite paper to be taken at one sitting.
scheme_of_examination.paper_1: Will consist of fifty multiple choice objective questions all of which must be answered within 1 hour for 40 marks.

paper_2.description: Will consist of nine essay-type questions. Candidates will be required to answer four questions within 2 hours for 60 marks.
sections: Section A: Themes from the Old Testament - four questions.
Section B: Themes from the Synoptic Gospels and the Acts of the Apostle - three questions.
Section C: Themes from selected Epistles two questions.
paper_2.instruction: Candidates will be required to answer four questions in all, choosing at least one question from each section and the fourth question from either Section A or Section B.
paper_2.bible_version: The Revised Standard Version of the Bible will be used in setting of questions.

section_a_old_testament.title: The Sovereignty of God
notes: (b) God, the Controller of the universe - Gen. 1; 2

section_a_old_testament.title: Submission to the will of God
notes: (a) David's submission to the will of God - 1 Sam 26;
(b) David's sin, repentance and forgiveness - 2 Sam 12: 15-25

section_a_old_testament.title: Making Decisions
notes: (b) The unwise decision of Solomon and Rehoboam - 1 Kings 9: 15-23; 11: 1-13; 12: 1-20

section_a_old_testament.title: Supremacy of God
notes: (a) Religious tensions during Ahab's reign - 1 Kings 16: 29-34; 17: 1-7; 18: 1-19

section_a_old_testament.title: Religious reforms
notes: The reign of King Josiah - 2 Kings 22; 23: 1-30

section_a_old_testament.title: Concern for one's Nation
notes: (a) The condition of the nation - 2 Kings 24; 25: 1-17
(b) Response to the state of the nation - Nehemiah 1; 2: 9-20; 4

section_a_old_testament.title: Nature of God
notes: (a) True religion and social justice - Amos - Amos 2: 6-8; 4; 5: 1-24; 7: 10-17; 8

section_b_synoptic_gospels_and_acts.title: The Call and Demands of Discipleship
The Trials of Jesus
notes: (c) Condemnation of Jesus - Matthew 26: 47-75; Mark 14: 53-72; Luke 22: 66-71; 23: 1-12

section_b_synoptic_gospels_and_acts.title: The Crucifixion, Burial and Resurrection of Jesus
notes: (a) Crucifixion and burial - Matthew 27: 32-66; Mark 15: 16-47; Luke 23: 26-56

section_b_synoptic_gospels_and_acts.title: The Holy Spirit and the Mission to the Gentiles
notes: (a) The Holy Spirit at Pentecost - Acts 2

Dickson, Kwesi: From the Institution of the Monarchy to the fall of the Northern Kingdom
